title: URLs & Domain Names
summary: A URL is the full address of one exact thing on the web.
The domain name is the main, memorable part, like example.com.
A URL has parts: the protocol, the domain, and the path.
The path points to a specific page inside the website.

sections.text: The address bar at the top of your browser holds more than it looks. Every part of that address has a job, and once you can read it, you always know where you are on the web.

sections.title: What is a URL?
sections.text: URL stands for Uniform Resource Locator. It is the full address that points to one exact thing on the web, like a page, an image, or a file. You type it or click it, and the browser knows exactly where to go.

sections.title: What is a Domain Name?
sections.text: The domain name is the main, memorable part of a URL, like example.com. It is the name of the website itself. One domain can hold many pages, and each page has its own full URL.

media.alt: A URL with the domain name highlighted

sections.title: A Home Address
sections.text: A URL is like a full postal address. The domain is the city and street that tells you the general place. The path that comes after is like the house number, pointing to the exact home you want to reach.

media.alt: A full address zooming from a city down to one house

sections.title: The Parts of a URL
items: The protocol comes first, like https, and sets the rules for the connection.
The domain name comes next, like example.com, naming the website.
The path follows, like /about, pointing to a specific page.
Extra parts can come after to carry small bits of information.
Together they form one address that leads to exactly one place.

sections.text: The dots and slashes in a URL are not decoration. Each one separates a part, so the browser can read the address piece by piece.

terms.definition: The full address that points to one exact thing on the web.
The main, memorable name of a website, like example.com.
The first part of a URL that sets the rules, like https.
The part of a URL that points to a specific page.
The box at the top of the browser where the URL appears.

options: The full address that points to one exact thing on the web
questions.explanation: A URL is the complete web address that leads to one exact page, image, or file.

questions.text: Which part is the memorable name of the website?
questions.explanation: The domain name, like example.com, is the main memorable part that names the website.

questions.text: In the address example, what does the path act like?
options: The house number pointing to the exact home
questions.explanation: The path is like the house number. It points to the exact page inside the website.
